record.field: Akaunting is a self-hosted, open-source accounting platform that gives small businesses and freelancers full control over their finances and data.

Akaunting is a self-hosted, open-source accounting platform that gives small businesses and freelancers full control over their finances and data.

Built on Laravel, Vue and Tailwind, it covers the full money-management cycle: invoicing, billing, income and expense tracking, double-entry bookkeeping and financial reporting. Its modular architecture and built-in App Store let you extend the core with additional features such as payroll, custom payment gateways and integrations. It supports multiple companies and currencies from a single install.

Key features
  • Invoicing, billing and quotes with recurring transactions
  • Income, expense and bank-transaction tracking with reconciliation
  • Double-entry bookkeeping with chart of accounts and journal entries
  • Customer and vendor management plus a client portal
  • Multi-company and multi-currency support from one instance
  • Financial reports (profit & loss, balance sheet, cash flow, tax) and a modular App Store
Best for

Small businesses, freelancers and accountants who want a self-hosted alternative to cloud accounting suites, keeping financial data on their own infrastructure. Note the core uses the Business Source License, which limits free production use to a small scale; commercial on-premise plans are available for larger deployments.

services: akaunting: image: docker.io/akaunting/akaunting:latest restart: unless-stopped depends_on: akaunting-db: condition: service_healthy environment: APP_URL: ${APP_URL} LOCALE: {{ LOCALE }} DB_HOST: akaunting-db DB_PORT: 3306 DB_NAME: {{ DB_NAME }} DB_USERNAME: ${DB_USERNAME} DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD} DB_PREFIX: {{ DB_PREFIX }} COMPANY_NAME: {{ COMPANY_NAME }} COMPANY_EMAIL: ${COMPANY_EMAIL} ADMIN_EMAIL: ${ADMIN_EMAIL} ADMIN_PASSWORD: ${ADMIN_PASSWORD} expose: - '80' volumes: - ./akaunting_data:/var/www/html container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network akaunting-db: image: mariadb:10.11 restart: unless-stopped environment: MYSQL_DATABASE: ${MYSQL_DATABASE} MYSQL_USER: ${MYSQL_USER} MYSQL_PASSWORD: ${MYSQL_PASSWORD} MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD: 'yes' volumes: - ./akaunting_db:/var/lib/mysql healthcheck: test: - CMD - mysqladmin - ping - -h - localhost interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_akaunting_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T akaunting-db sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d akaunting-db # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T akaunting-db sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
